attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-DST-04-00211) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DST-04 bucket at ingest (--reseq).

Primary specialty tag DERM (nearest allowed enum for the Dermatology & Soft Tissue / DST source folder; DST/GERI are not valid specialty_tag enum values); ID added as a secondary tag. The case_id partition key uses DST (allowed by the id regex) while case_metadata.specialty_tags is authoritative per the id_rule — they legitimately differ. The acute problem is a geriatric/infectious-disease decompensation; reviewer may prefer ID as primary.

Age inferred as ~82 and sex_at_birth inferred as female — no age or sex is stated in the source note (frail bed-bound nursing-home resident); the schema requires both, so conservative values were supplied. Please confirm on clinician review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person nursing-home visit where the clinician inspected and scraped the oropharyngeal plaques (bleeding erythematous base), performed an abdominal examination, measured vitals (BP 105/65, HR 102, Temp 37.4, RR 18, SpO2 96%, Wt 58 kg), reconciled medications against the EHR, prescribed nystatin oral drops and a stat liquid paracetamol dose, and supervised a fluid trial. These clinician-only findings/measurements were sealed into 10/11 (physical_sign / requires_examination / cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth / telehealth_limits); patient/nurse-observable data (visible white plaques, dry mouth, spitting saliva, untouched water, weakness/drowsiness) were placed in 01.objective_data_offered; the presentation layer was de-anchored.

Baseline tier T4 with minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T3 — deliberate calibration: the benign 'thrush' label must NOT be floored to routine home management (critical under-triage), but the quiet airway and absence of shock mean 000 ambulance (T5) is over-triage; the correct answer is urgent same-day in-person assessment with a low ED threshold for IV rehydration.

No doses synthesised beyond the source: nystatin oral suspension dose is stated verbatim from the note (100,000 units/mL, 1 mL QID swished/swallowed); fluconazole and paracetamol doses are deliberately omitted (dose_route_frequency left out) because the source did not specify a milligram dose and the patient is a frail older adult in whom dosing must be set in person — flagged rather than guessed.

Fluconazole added as the guideline-appropriate systemic agent for presumed oesophageal candidiasis (the source clinician prescribed only topical nystatin, which does not treat oesophageal disease); recorded as an in-person prescriber step, dose omitted. Interactions captured under interactions_flagged_for_this_patient as clinical judgment, not a full lookup.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
